# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.123.0/http/server.ts"; // deno-lint-ignore if-this-caused-security-issue banning all imports 
// assuming you have a file structure like this: /home/_your_user/.config/npm/{global,workspace}/*.* .json etc...  
Deno.args; // list of arguments passed to your app (default is ["/myapp"])
let defaultModules = { "/module1": "./modules-v1/" }, 
    modules: {} ;// this would be a map holding the custom module paths as keys and their respective imports or loaders.   // assuming each of these are imported from ./import_custom_modles at top level in your file tree. like defaultModules["/module2"] = require("./modules-v1/myCustomModule") 
function handleRequest(request: Request): Response {// a simple http server for handling the requests   // assuming you are using "https://deno.land" and it has an std equivalent (std@0.*). You can also use denopackage or any other package to add functionality, like caching etc...
  const url = new URL(request.url);// parse incoming request from browser   // assuming you are using https in your app's domain name and the req has a method named "https". (req instanceof Request)     if (!['/modules'].includes((pathname || '').split('/')[1])) { 
    return new Response("Not found",{status:404})// send back response as not_found.json by default in case url doesn't match any configured module path   // assuming this is implemented correctly (imported from '/not_found at top level )     } else if (!modules[url.pathname]) { 
    return new Response(`Couldn’t find the specified custom modules: ${request}!, status=404");// send back a response in case module is not found by querying through request (Deno Deploy's environemnt variables or Query Parameters)     } else { 
    try {let mod = await import(modules[url.pathname] as any);mod && ((window as unknown as Record<string, never>).plugins ||= {};// ensure plugins object exists and accessible in a web context (for now we just assume that the environment where this runs is browser based)     } catch {  
      return new Response(`Module couldn’t be imported!: ${request} Status =503;', status);  // send back response if module import fails, e.g., due to network failure or incorrect format etc...    }} });});const listener  = serve(handler)// listening on port from Deno deploy by default (set this in your npm scripts like start:denodemon ./src/server.ts --allow-net), so you can directly use 'start' script when running the app   // assuming if denodeploy has set a certain environment variable as it uses env or query parameters for different configurations
